root_text.sn12.58:1.2: “Saṁyojaniyesu, bhikkhave, dhammesu assādānupassino viharato nāmarūpassa avakkanti hoti.
root_text.sn12.58:2.2: Tassa yāni ceva mūlāni adhogamāni, yāni ca tiriyaṅgamāni, sabbāni tāni uddhaṁ ojaṁ abhiharanti.
root_text.sn12.58:2.3: Evañhi so, bhikkhave, mahārukkho tadāhāro tadupādāno ciraṁ dīghamaddhānaṁ tiṭṭheyya.
root_text.sn12.58:2.4: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, saṁyojaniyesu dhammesu assādānupassino viharato nāmarūpassa avakkanti hoti …pe….
root_text.sn12.58:3.1: Saṁyojaniyesu, bhikkhave, dhammesu ādīnavānupassino viharato nāmarūpassa avakkanti na hoti.
root_text.sn12.58:4.4: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, saṁyojaniyesu dhammesu ādīnavānupassino viharato nāmarūpassa avakkanti na hoti.

translation_text.sn12.58:1.2: “There are things that tighten the fetters. When you concentrate on the gratification provided by these things, name and form are conceived.
translation_text.sn12.58:1.3: Name and form are requirements for the six sense fields. …
translation_text.sn12.58:1.4: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.58:2.1: Suppose there was a great tree.
translation_text.sn12.58:2.2: And its roots going downwards and across all draw the sap upwards.
translation_text.sn12.58:2.3: Fed and fuelled by that, the great tree would stand for a long time.
translation_text.sn12.58:2.4: In the same way, there are things that tighten the fetters. When you concentrate on the gratification provided by these things, name and form are conceived. …
translation_text.sn12.58:3.1: There are things that tighten the fetters. When you concentrate on the drawbacks of these things, name and form are not conceived.
translation_text.sn12.58:3.2: When name and form cease, the six sense fields cease. …
translation_text.sn12.58:3.3: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.
translation_text.sn12.58:4.1: Suppose there was a great tree.
translation_text.sn12.58:4.2: Then a person comes along with a spade and basket. …
translation_text.sn12.58:4.3: In this way the great tree is cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.sn12.58:4.4: In the same way, there are things that tighten the fetters. When you concentrate on the drawbacks of these things, name and form are not conceived.
translation_text.sn12.58:4.5: When name and form cease, the six sense fields cease. …
translation_text.sn12.58:4.6: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.”
